apis.name: @ohos.multimedia.image
apis.purpose: Image processing module: decode, encode, edit, metadata, image receiving

apis.name: ImageSource
apis.purpose: Image decoding source: create from uri/fd/ArrayBuffer, decode to PixelMap/Picture, get image info

apis.name: PixelMap
apis.purpose: Bitmap object with pixel data; supports scale, crop, rotate, flip, translate, and color space operations

apis.name: createPixelMap
apis.purpose: Create PixelMap from pixel data buffer or decode from ImageSource (multiple overloads: async/sync, with/without allocator type)

apis.name: getImageInfo
apis.purpose: Get ImageInfo (width, height, mimeType) from either ImageSource or PixelMap

notableFindings: Import is `import { image } from '@kit.ImageKit';` — not from `@kit.ImageKit` directly, the module is `image` within ImageKit.
For avatar thumbnails, prefer `ImageSource.createPixelMap({ desiredSize })` to decode at target size directly, and/or use `createThumbnail()` for JPEG/HEIF which prioritizes embedded thumbnails.
For image resize, decode with desiredSize for memory efficiency, then use `pixelMap.scale()` or `createScaledPixelMap()` for further resizing.
Always call `.release()` on ImageSource and PixelMap to avoid OOM — images are large native objects.
PixelMap serialization max 128MB; PixelMap supports Worker cross-thread transfer from API 11.
`image.createPixelMap()` (old global, API 8) is deprecated from API 26 in favor of `createPixelMapFromPixels()`.
The old `@ohos.multimedia.image` module name is effectively replaced by the `@kit.ImageKit` kit import, with identical API surface.
`createThumbnail()` supports JPEG and HEIF only; for other formats, decode with desiredSize directly.
